item.title: Not specified
item.description:
SLOANE HELICOPTERS LIMITED
STANDARD CONDITIONS FOR THE SALE OF GOODS AND SERVICES
1. INTRODUCTION
1.1. In these Terms and Conditions the “Company” means Sloane Helicopters Limited and the
“Customer” means any person, firm or body offering to buy goods (the “Goods”) and/or
services (the “Services”) from the Company.
1.2. Unless otherwise agreed in writing by an authorised representative of the Company, these
conditions constitute the only conditions upon which the Company is willing to supply Goods
and/or Services.
1.3. These conditions shall prevail over any terms and/or conditions in the Customer’s order or any
other document issued by the Customer except where specifically agreed in writing to by an
authorised representative of the Company.
1.4. The Customer’s attention is drawn particularly to Conditions 6.1, 8.3, 9 and 10 which
exclude or limit the Company’s liability.
2. QUOTATIONS AND ACCEPTANCE OF ORDER
2.1. Any order given by the Customer in respect of a quotation by the Company, shall not be
binding on the Company until accepted in writing by it.
2.2. The Company’s price lists and estimates do not constitute offers made by the Company and, in
any event, the Company may at its absolute discretion refuse to accept any order. The
acceptance by the Company of each separate order shall constitute a separate contract.
2.3. Quotations given by the Company shall be valid for 30 days only, unless otherwise stated.
3. PRICE
3.1. Save where otherwise specified, all prices are net and subject, where applicable, to the
addition of VAT at the applicable rate and, in the case of export sales to the Customer, any
applicable customs, import and export and similar duties, and all prices are exclusive of
carriage and insurance.
3.2. The Company shall have the right, subject to reasonable prior notice, to vary the prices quoted
in the event of any increase in the cost of materials and/or labour and/or increase in customs,
import or export duties.
3.3. Where any prices or charges are given and/or where the Company is involved in expenditure in
a currency other than sterling, the Company shall be entitled to adjust its prices to compensate
for any change in the rate of exchange of such currency in relation to sterling by reason of
which the costs to the Company have increased.
3.4. In the event that the Company shall store any of the Goods for any period after the expiry of 7
days from the date upon which written notice has been served on the Customer that such
Goods are ready for collection, the Customer shall pay the Company its reasonable charges for
storage of such Goods.
3.5. In the event of any variation or suspension of the work caused by the Customer’s instructions
or lack of instructions the Company shall be entitled to adjust the price to reflect any additional
costs incurred by the Company and to adjust delivery dates or schedules.
SHL standard T&Cs – 9/2010
1
3.6. Where the Company provides Services, the Customer shall bear the cost of any testing of the
Customer’s aircraft, components or equipment that is necessary.
4. PAYMENT
4.1. The Company shall be entitled to receive payment in full in the currency of the invoice on
presentation of an invoice (without any right of set-off, counterclaim, deduction or withholding
whatsoever). Unless otherwise agreed by the Company in writing, all payments shall be paid to
the Company’s account maintained with National Westminster Bank plc, 40 Market Street,
Wellingborough, Northamptonshire NN8 1AD UK Sorting Code: 55-70-37, Account No:
90939131 for Sterling payments; Sorting Code: 60-73-01, Account No: 02662663 for US
Dollar payments; Sorting Code: 60-72-11, Account No: 84000376 for Euro payments. As a
condition of supplying Goods and/or Services, or further Goods and/or Services, the Company
may require a payment on account or in advance. Time for payment of sums due to the
Company shall be of the essence.
4.2. The Company shall be entitled to receive interest on any sum owed by the Customer from the
date that sum was due to be paid. Such interest shall accrue and be calculated on a daily basis
both before and after any judgment at the rate of 4% above the base rate from time to time of
National Westminster Bank plc (or in its absence a reasonable equivalent), or the US Prime
Rate, as applicable, for the period from the due date for its payment until the date on which it
is actually paid and shall be compounded monthly.
4.3. If the Customer disputes an invoice in good faith it shall provide the Company with written
details of the dispute element within 14 days of the invoice and pay the undisputed part in
accordance with Condition 4.1. Failure to comply shall result in a deemed acceptance of the
invoice in question.
5. DELIVERY
5.1. Any quoted delivery or completion date is the Company’s best estimate and not a contractual
commitment. The Company fulfils its obligation to deliver when it makes the Goods available to
the Customer or the Customer’s agent for collection at the Company’s premises. At the
request of the Customer and at the Customer’s expense, the Company will arrange for the
carriage of Goods to a location other than the Company’s premises, but the Company shall
have no liability for any loss or damage to the Goods while in transit nor for any act or
omission (negligent or otherwise) of any third party in connection with such carriage.
5.2. Risk in the Goods shall pass to the Customer upon delivery at the Company’s premises or, if
applicable, on despatch of the goods to the Customer.
5.3. In the event that the Customer fails to take delivery of any Goods within 7 days of the
Company giving written notice that they are ready for collection, the Company shall be
entitled, at its sole discretion, to store such Goods at the Customer’s expense and/or to resell
all or any part of such Goods without prejudice to any other night or remedy of the Company.
5.4. Where delivery of Goods to the Customer is to be by installment, each installment shall
constitute a separate contract. Any defect or failure in delivery of one or more installments
shall not entitle the Customer to cancel any other installment.
6. CUSTOMER’S PROPERTY
6.1. Any property of the Customer placed in the Company’s possession shall be held and handled
entirely at the Customer’s risk without any liability on the part of the Company for any loss or
damage unless caused by an act or omission of the Company done with intent to cause
damage or recklessly and with knowledge that damage would probably result; provided that, in
the case of such act or omission of either an employee or agent of the Company, the Company
shall be liable only if he or she was acting within the scope of his or her employment or agency
2
(as the case may be). In no circumstances shall the Company be liable for any economic,
consequential, or indirect loss or damage suffered by the Customer.
6.2. The Company shall be entitled to a general and particular lien on any of the Customer’s goods
(including any aircraft or engine records) in the Company’s possession for all amounts
whatsoever and howsoever due from the Customer to the Company, even though possession
of such goods may have been relinquished by the Company from time to time. Unless informed
otherwise, the Company shall be entitled to assume that the Customer is entitled to subject
such goods to the Company’s lien. The Company may seek to recover from the Customer all
the costs and expenses of exercising its lien including (without limitation) storage charges.
6.3. If any amount due from the Customer is outstanding for more than 30 days of a written
demand for payment being made by the Company, the Company may sell on such terms and
at such price as it considers reasonable any of the Customer’s goods in its possession. The
Customer shall give to the Company such assistance, including the delivery of any relevant
documents, for the purpose of completing such sale. The Company shall apply the proceeds of
sale firstly in payment of all costs and expenses incurred by the Company in connection with
the sale, including (without limitation) storage, management time, legal and other professional
costs and charges and secondly in total or partial satisfaction of such amount owed by the
Customer. In the event of any shortfall, the Customer shall pay such shortfall on demand. In
the event of a surplus, the Company shall pay such excess to the Customer.
7. PASSING OF PROPERTY
7.1. Title to the Goods supplied (whether on their own or with or as part of performance of
Services) by the Company to the Customer shall remain with the Company until full payment
has been received by the Company whereupon the Customer shall take the Goods with full title
guarantee and in the event that possession of any Goods has been given to the Customer
before title has passed, the Customer shall hold such Goods as bailee and shall store them in
such a way as to enable them to be identifiable as property of the Company and not encumber
them in any way until full payment has been made.
7.2. While acting as bailee of any Goods, the Customer shall on demand immediately notify the
Company of the whereabouts of the Goods and give the Company free access to them.
7.3. In the event that the Company gives the Customer written notice that the Company has
reasonable grounds for believing that any of the events set out in paragraph 11 below has
occurred or is about to occur, the Customer’s authority to possess the Goods of which it is
bailee shall automatically end and all such Goods shall be immediately re-delivered to the
Company.
7.4. As bailor of the Goods, the Company, either acting itself or through any agent, shall be entitled
to enter upon or into any land, buildings, vehicle or aircraft where the Goods or part of them
are situated or reasonably thought to be situated and may re-take possession of them at any
time. If the Goods have been fitted to or fixed to an engine or aircraft, the Company shall
have the right to take possession of such engine or aircraft until the Goods have been
detached. The Company’s title in the Goods shall not be affected by any stipulation or rule of
law that the Goods become part of an engine or aircraft.
7.5. In the case of parts or components removed or replaced by the Company in the performance
of Services, such parts or components shall become the property of the Company.
8. WARRANTY
8.1. The Company warrants that it will perform all Services in accordance with all applicable
regulatory requirements.
8.2. The Company will use its reasonable endeavours to pass on to the Customer (at the
Customer’s cost) the benefit of any warranty in respect of the Goods given by any third party.
3
8.3. Save as expressly provided in this Condition, any Goods purchased by the Customer are sold
“as is” (i.e. in their actual state and condition at the time of delivery) and the Company makes
no warranty or representation as to value, design or performance and all warranties,
representations, conditions, obligations and liability of any kind whatsoever, whether in
contract or tort whether express or implied, or otherwise in respect of the Goods are expressly
excluded. Additionally, the General Product Safety Regulations 1994 shall not apply to any
Goods supplied for repair or reconditioning before use.
8.4. Where Goods are described as “new” such term shall mean that the Goods are unused, save
for testing.
8.5. If the Customer establishes to the Company’s reasonable satisfaction within 14 days of the
date of completion of the Services that there was faulty workmanship in such Services, the
Company shall at its option either credit the Customer with the price paid in respect of the
Services or remedy the defect at its own cost provided that any Goods concerned shall (where
applicable) have been
in accordance with the
manufacturer’s recommendations and good airline and engineering practice without repair or
alteration not approved by the Company and provided the Goods concerned have not been
the subject of any accident or foreign object damage or abuse or misappropriation, and provided
always that the Customer bears the cost of returning the Goods to the Company.
installed, operated and maintained
8.6. If the Customer establishes to the Company’s reasonable satisfaction within 14 days of the
date of any expression of opinion or giving of advice by the Company that there was a material
error in any expression of fact which the Company should have been aware with the exercise
of due diligence, the Company shall credit the Customer with the price paid in respect of such
opinion or advice.
9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY AND INDEMNITY
9.1. Nothing in these Conditions shall exclude or restrict any legal liability of the Company for death
or personal injury resulting from the negligence of the Company or restrict any of the
Company’s legal obligations arising under Section 12 of the Sale of Goods Act 1979 or under
the Consumer Protection Act 1987 or in respect of fraud. Additionally, where the Company is
dealing with a consumer his or her statutory rights are not affected by this Condition 9.
9.2. Save as provided in Condition 8, the Company shall have no liability to the Customer for any
damages or losses (indirect, direct or consequential) resulting from defects in design, materials
or workmanship or statement of opinion or from any act or default of the Company (whether
negligent or otherwise) unless caused intentionally or recklessly by the Company.
9.3. Save as provided in Condition 8 and 9.2, the Company’s aggregate liability to the Customer
whether for negligence or breach of contract, misrepresentation or otherwise shall in those
circumstances not exceed the cost of the Goods and/or Services which give rise to such liability
in respect of any occurrence or series of occurrences.
10.FORCE MAJEURE
10.1.
The Company shall not be liable for the loss, damage or otherwise as a direct or indirect
result of the failure to perform or delay in performing any of its obligations or be in breach of
an agreement with the Customer as a result of the occurrence of any circumstance or event
beyond its control, including without limitation failure to receive or delay in receipt of the
Customer’s Goods, acts of God, fire, flood, storm, civil disturbance, acts of terrorism,
explosion, power failure or reduction of power supplies, acts, orders or requirements of any
governmental or regulatory body, lack or shortage of materials or parts, inability to procure or
delay in procuring equipment and materials from its normal suppliers, mechanical breakdown
or strike, lock-out or labour dispute.
4
10.2.
If the Company cannot complete any Services for any such reasons as is mentioned in
Condition 10 it shall be entitled to be paid by the Customer in proportion to the amount of
work done in accordance with Condition 4.
10.3.
The Company shall as soon as reasonably practicable give the Customer notice of any
such event as is mentioned in Condition 10.1 which causes the Company to be unable to
perform its obligations on time or at all.
11.FINANCIAL CONDITION OF CUSTOMER
11.1.
On the occurrence of any of the events listed at (a) to (g) below, all sums outstanding to
the Company shall become immediately due and payable and the Company may elect to
proceed with the performance of contracts subsisting with the Customer or to treat such
contracts as discharged. In the case of the latter, the Customer shall remain liable to
indemnify the Company against all costs incurred by the Company in connection with such
contracts until their discharge.
a. The Customer breaches any of its obligations to the Company.
b. The Customer wrongfully stops payment of any debt or is deemed to be unable to pay its
debts (within the meaning of Section 123 of the Insolvency Act 1986).
c. A receiver, liquidator, trustee, encumbrances of similar officer is appointed over the whole
or any substantial part of the Customer’s undertaking, property or assets or if a petition is
presented for the appointment of an administrator of the Customer.
d. The security of any of the Customer’s secured obligations is enforced or any distress,
execution, sequestration or other process is levied or enforced on or taken out against the
Customer.
e. The Customer enters into or offers to enter into any arrangements or composition for the
benefit of its creditors.
f. The Customer being an individual dies, is declared bankrupt or becomes of unsound mind or
a patient for the purposes of any statute referring to mental health.
g. Provisions equivalent to (c), (d), (e) or (f) in any other system of law apply to the
12.GENERAL
12.1.
The Company shall be entitled to sub-contract performance of the whole or part of any
agreement governed by these Conditions without prior notice to or the consent of the
Customer. In any agreement governed by these Conditions the Company contracts for and on
behalf of itself and its sub-contractor.
12.2.
If any part of any provision of these Conditions or any agreement governed by these
Conditions shall be invalid or unenforceable, the remainder of such provision and all other
provisions of these Conditions or any agreement governed by these Conditions shall remain
valid and enforceable to the fullest extent permitted by law.
12.3.
No amendment or variation of these Conditions or any agreement governed by these
Conditions shall be effective unless it is made or confirmed in a written document signed by a
Director of the Company and the Customer.
12.4.
The parties to this Agreement do not

item.title: B1.3 Licensed Engineer
item.description: Sloane Helicopters has a long and excellent reputation for providing helicopter EMS services and has been providing air ambulance support since 1999. Due to continued expansion, we now have an exciting opportunity for a B1.3 Licensed Engineer to join the Sloane Helicopters team in support of the Essex & Herts Air Ambulance, based at North Weald and Earls Colne. This is a fantastic opportunity to utilise and develop your existing skills to make a real difference in the world and to play a vital part in saving lives. Reporting to the Line Maintenance Supervisor, this is a unique position for an engineer who is happy to work on their own initiative with a great deal of autonomy whilst, at the same time, being part of an experienced and supportive team. The successful candidate will hold a B1.3 licence and, although not essential, ideally with type-ratings for AW169/AW109 aircraft. They will also need the personal discipline to maintain the aircraft in their care to a consistent high standard. Working at Sloane is not just a job. Becoming part of the Sloane team will give you a real sense of belonging and you will be joining at a very exciting point in the company’s continued evolution. We want to nurture and develop our employees to ensure they reach their full potential and to help drive the company forward to greater success. In addition to a competitive salary, you will also be eligible to be enrolled on the new Employee Bonus Scheme which will grow in direct correlation with the company’s success. Due to the mobile nature of this role, you will also be supplied with a company van. Other benefits will include: Contributory Pension Scheme – 5% company contribution, Private Healthcare, Death in Service – 3x salary, Income Protection Scheme. If using your skills and experience to help save lives sounds like the role that you have been waiting for, please forward your CV by email, together with details of your current salary, to Mrs Andrea Peel - apeel@sloanehelicopters.com Your CALLING Charity Needs B1.3 LICENSED ENGINEERS YOU!

item.title: Ground Operations Officer
item.description: The main purpose of the Ground Operations Officer is to help and assist the pilots and act as the link between NHV Denmark and the client. Main Responsibilities Make all the preparation for flights Responsible for planning and coordination for all NHV Denmark’s flight operations Understand the client´s business and needs, under the decision and supervision of the Ground Operations Manager Liaise with customers as required/directed on delays and significant events. Assist with customer enquires or queries as required. Liaise with line internal departments and customers as required on current operational matters. Pilots scheduling Ensure all administration is carried out to the required standard and regulations Anticipate and co-ordinate required changes to the flight schedule and liaise with relevant departments. Assist the colleagues and other ad hoc work from Operation or Ground Operations Manager Responsible for complying with Information Security Management System (ISMS) policies, procedures, and controls and supporting information security objectives Skills and Experience Skils Service minded Language: Good knowledge of Danish and English is mandatory Competent in the use of IT is mandatory Good at communications Ability to work under pressure Experience Experience in aviation environment is preferred Experience in operations or coordination is preferred Is a team player and helps out colleagues where needed Competent in the use of IT is mandatory Language: Good knowledge of Danish and English is mandatory

item.title: AW139 Co-Pilot
item.description:
NHV Group is a European based helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore.
Are you an experienced AW139 Co-Pilot ready to operate in a professional offshore environment? At our Den Helder base, you will perform scheduled shuttle flights supporting offshore Oil & Gas operations, ensuring the highest standards of safety, efficiency and customer service. You will operate in a highly structured and safety-driven environment where professionalism and teamwork are essential.
Please note: Due to operational requirements, candidates must reside within reasonable commuting distance of the Den Helder base.
Your Role As an AW139 Co-Pilot, you are responsible for executing flights in the safest and most cost-effective manner, in full compliance with aviation regulations, company procedures and operational standards.
What You’ll Be Doing Flight Preparation & Execution Execute flights in accordance with SOPs, limitations and performance requirements Assisting the pilot-in-command with administrative flight duties Understanding the client’s business & needs, under the decision and supervision of the Chief Pilot Compliance & Professional Standards Maintain full familiarity with national and international aviation legislation, agreed aviation practices and local procedures Operate in accordance with the Operations Manual, FOM, FOIs and company procedures Respect company policies including Code of Conduct, Safety, Quality and Drugs & Alcohol policies Support the pilot-in-command in maintaining a proper standard of crew discipline, conduct and personal appearance Offshore & Team Responsibilities Act responsible to the pilot-in-command in assisting to conduct an efficient flight Support offshore Oil & Gas operations as assigned by the Chief Pilot Remain flexible for operational needs Lead by example and promote team spirit Adapt to local culture, environment and client needs
What You Bring Qualifications EASA CPL(H) License - mandatory UK CPL(H) License - desirable EASA Class 1 Medical - mandatory UK CAA Class 1 Medical - desirable Multi-Engine Instrument Rating - mandatory AW139 Type Rating English Proficiency Level 4 Total expected flight hours: Min. 500 hours total flight time on helicopters Min. 500 hours Multi-engine helicopter Competencies Strong ICT skills (aviation software & administrative systems) Dutch language skills (preferred but not mandatory) Safety-first mindset Professional, customer-oriented attitude Flexible and operationally resilient (e.g. for double crewing, standby storm duties ...) Team player Living within commuting distance of Den Helder
Join the Thrilling World of Aviation Become part of an international helicopter services company that combines a youthful mindset with over 25 years of industry expertise. We operate in a highly professional offshore environment where teamwork, safety and technical precision are paramount. What We Offer Permanent (indefinite) contract Competitive salary package 14 days ON / 14 days OFF roster 183 duties per year Early/Late shift pattern A professional and experienced offshore aviation team 📲If you are an AW139-rated co-pilot who thrives in structured offshore operations and takes pride in delivering safe, precise and customer-focused flights — and you are locally based near Den Helder — we would love to hear from you!

item.title: Technical Director
item.description:
NHV Group is a European based helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore.
We are seeking an experienced and strategic Technical Director to lead and oversee all technical helicopter operations across our multi-base organization. Reporting directly to the CEO and serving on the Extended Executive Committee, you will hold full accountability for Maintenance (Part-145), CAMO, Supply Chain, Logistics, Fleet Management, and Part-147 training. You will ensure safe, compliant, efficient, and customer-focused technical operations while driving performance, cost control, and fleet availability. This is a high-impact executive role combining operational leadership with strong business acumen and full P&L responsibility. You will lead technical transformation initiatives including fleet optimization, digitalization, predictive maintenance, and sustainability programs, positioning the organization for future growth.
This function can be performed from any of the NHV bases. Regular travel to other bases is required.
Responsibilities and areas Operational & Strategic Leadership Lead and optimize all technical functions (Part-145 Maintenance, CAMO, Engineering, Supply Chain, Logistics, Fleet Management, Part-147) with a strong business-driven and performance-focused approach. Develop and execute technical strategies, structures, and processes aligned with corporate objectives, ensuring efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and operational excellence. Drive organizational optimization and continuous improvement initiatives across maintenance and supply chain operations. Ensure technical capabilities fully support operational delivery, customer commitments, and strategic growth objectives. Establish, monitor, and report on KPIs covering safety, compliance, service quality, cost efficiency, and fleet availability. Ensure compliance with ISMS policies and execute additional executive-level technical assignments as required.
Financial & Performance Management Hold full P&L responsibility for technical operations, including maintenance budgets, supply chain expenditures, and lifecycle cost management. Drive sustainable cost optimization and total cost of ownership improvements across the fleet. Partner with Finance to ensure disciplined budgeting, forecasting, financial oversight, and structured performance reporting. Balance profitability objectives with uncompromising safety and regulatory compliance standards.
People Leadership & Culture Provide strategic leadership to multi-location technical teams, building strong leadership capability, succession planning, and skills development. Establish clear performance goals, accountability frameworks, and KPI-driven management structures. Foster a culture aligned with company values, embedding safety, accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Promote strong cross-functional alignment and transparent executive communication.
Safety, Compliance & Governance Ensure full compliance with AOC, (B)CAA, EASA/CAA/ICAO regulations, and internal standards across all technical domains. Oversee governance of Accountable Managers and Postholders (Part-145, CAMO, Supply Chain, Fleet Management, Part-147). Maintain robust operational controls, reporting systems, documentation, approvals, and regulatory records. Champion airworthiness, safety, quality assurance, and consistent adherence to procedures and best practices.
Transformation, Innovation & Continuous Improvement Lead technical transformation initiatives including fleet optimization, digitalization, predictive maintenance, sustainability programs, and SAF adoption. Introduce innovative technologies and structured change management to enhance reliability, safety, availability, and cost efficiency. Drive continuous performance improvement across all technical and logistical functions.
Stakeholder Management Build and maintain strong relationships with OEMs, suppliers, MROs, regulators, aviation authorities, and customers. Represent the technical organization at executive and regulatory levels. Ensure external partnerships and contractual commitments align with internal technical strategy and support business growth.
Skills & Experience Extensive senior leadership experience in aviation technical management or a comparable highly regulated industry. Proven experience as (Nominated) Postholder under an AOC and/or as Accountable Manager. In-depth knowledge of aviation maintenance regulations and regulatory frameworks. Strong financial acumen, including P&L ownership and cost control. Demonstrated success in leading transformation, restructuring, and performance optimization initiatives. Experience in an international, multi-base operational environment. Solid understanding of business planning, operational management, and customer contract management. Results-driven, structured, and proactive mindset. Strong analytical capabilities with a KPI-focused approach to performance management. Professional level of English (spoken and written).
What we offer Join the Thrilling World of Aviation Step into an exciting career in an international helicopter services company that combines a youthful spirit with over 25 years of industry expertise. What We Offer When you join our team, you can count on a competitive salary package. This role includes regular travel to our other offices. The home base is not fixed. If you’re a experienced and strategic leader who thrives in a hands-on, fast-paced helicopter environment and takes pride in delivering safe, compliant, efficient, and customer-focused technical operations, we’d love to hear from you!

item.title: Flight Operations Officer
item.description: The Flight Operations Officer is responsible for the execution of the daily base flight schedule for our base in Den Helder. They will interface with a number of internal and external stakeholders to manage the safe, compliant and efficient delivery of the program, whilst upholding a high standard of service to NHV’s clients. The position is an operational as well as administrative function, part of the operations department, and reports directly to the Service Delivery Lead. This is a part-time function (20 hours / week). Depending on the candidate and preferences, this could be extended. Responsibilities and areas Flight Planning & Coordination Prepare and maintain daily flight schedules, including flight watch and monitoring. Anticipate and coordinate required changes to the flight schedule. Provide operational paperwork and briefings to flight crews. Monitor weather and NOTAMs relevant to flight operations. Crew Management Manage pilot scheduling and ensure compliance with Flight Time Limitations (FTL). Liaise with crew members regarding operational updates and changes. Client & Stakeholder Liaison Act as a key point of contact for clients regarding flight changes, disruptions, and queries. Liaise with Air Traffic Control and airport authorities as needed. Communicate with internal departments (Engineering, Ground Operations, etc.) to ensure fleet availability and operational readiness. Administration & Compliance Ensure all operational and customer reports are completed accurately and on time. Maintain high standards of documentation in line with regulatory and company requirements. Skills & experience Strong interest in aviation operations and flight coordination; previous experience is a plus but not required. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. Professional, friendly and customer-focused personality. Ability to work under pressure and adapt to changing circumstances. Proficiency in scheduling tools and operational software. Fluent in Dutch and English (spoken and written). Willingness to work flexible hours, including weekends and holidays. What we offer You'll be welcomed in a young and exciting company. Based on your skills and competences, you'll be offered a competitive salary with extra legal benefits. The opportunity can be taken to widen your knowledge and skills.

item.title: AW139 Captain
item.description:
NHV Group is a European based helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore.
Are you an experienced AW139 Captain ready to operate in a professional offshore environment? At our Den Helder base, you will perform scheduled shuttle flights supporting offshore Oil & Gas operations, ensuring the highest standards of safety, efficiency and customer service. You will operate in a highly structured and safety-driven environment where professionalism and teamwork are essential.
Please note: Due to operational requirements, candidates must reside within reasonable commuting distance of the Den Helder base.
Your Role As an AW139 Captain, you are responsible for executing flights in the safest and most cost-effective manner, in full compliance with aviation regulations, company procedures and operational standards. You report to the Chief Pilot and play a key role in representing the company towards our offshore clients.
What You’ll Be Doing Flight Preparation & Execution Perform full technical preparation of flights (route planning, fuel calculations, aircraft inspection) Execute flights in accordance with SOPs, limitations and performance requirements Ensure all aircraft safety systems are fully operational Complete accurate pre- and post-flight administration Report any incidents, defects or irregularities Compliance & Professional Standards Maintain full familiarity with national and international aviation legislation Operate in accordance with the Operations Manual, FOM, FOIs and company procedures Respect company policies including Safety, Quality and Drugs & Alcohol policies Maintain high standards of crew discipline, conduct and personal appearance Offshore & Team Responsibilities Support offshore Oil & Gas operations as assigned by the Chief Pilot Remain flexible for operational needs Demonstrate strong customer service awareness Lead by example and promote team spirit Adapt to local culture, environment and client needs
What You Bring Qualifications EASA ATPL(H) License - mandatory UK ATPL(H) License - desirable EASA Class 1 Medical - mandatory UK CAA Class 1 Medical – desirable Type Rating AW139 English Proficiency Level 4 Total expected flight hours 3000 total hours helicopter (but may be reduced by 1000 hours when total hours in similar aircraft complexity exceeds 1000 hours) 1500 total hours in command on helicopters (may include PICUS and up to 10% in a flight simulator) 1200 total hours in command multi-engine on helicopters (may include PICUS and up to 10% in a flight simulator) 500 hours similar complexity helicopter (may include up to 10% in a flight simulator) 100 hours in command on AW139 Competencies Good ICT-knowledge (specific aviation software & administrative programs) Knows & plays by the procedures & processes (operational, quality, safety...) installed by the company Flexible (e.g. for double crewing, standby storm duties ...), a team player, result driven and customer focused
What we offer Join the Thrilling World of Aviation Become part of an international helicopter services company that combines a youthful mindset with over 25 years of industry expertise. We operate in a highly professional offshore environment where teamwork, safety and technical precision are paramount. What We Offer Permanent (indefinite) contract Competitive salary package 14 days ON / 14 days OFF roster 183 duties per year Early/Late shift pattern A professional and experienced offshore aviation team 📲If you are an AW139-rated pilot who thrives in structured offshore operations and takes pride in delivering safe, precise and customer-focused flights — and you are locally based near Den Helder — we would love to hear from you!

item.title: Experienced HHO Operator – Licensed Engineer
item.description:
NHV Group is a European based helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore.
Are you an experienced Helicopter Hoist Operator and licensed engineer with a passion for safety and efficiency? Join our team to execute both scheduled and unscheduled Helicopter Hoist Operations (HHO) flights and crew change flights.
The helicopter Hoist Operator plays a critical role combining technical skills as a Licensed Line Engineer working on the helicopter and performing hoist flights hoisting passengers to the wind farms. Safety management and good communication play a crucial role.
You work in a small team of engineers, pilots and hoist operators following the helicopter to different operational locations. You report functionally to the Maintenance Lead who oversees the maintenance and hierarchically to the Base Manager in Esbjerg. You will follow the aircraft in bases in Denmark, the Netherlands, Germany, Poland, Belgium or any other European country the customer requests.
Responsibilities and Main Results Areas
Helicopter Hoist operator (HHO)
Carry out HHO flight duties, in accordance with NHV Operations SOP's.
Know the NHV procedures and the Flight Operations Manual.
Inspect hoist systems, cables, hooks, harnesses, and safety equipment.
Conduct safety briefings with passengers and flight crew.
Coordinate passenger- and aircraft handling procedures before and after flights.
Assist with the technical preparation for the flight(s).
Operate hoist controls smoothly to raise/lower personnel or cargo.
Provide precise directional guidance to the pilot (clearance, obstacles, positioning).
Immediately halt operations if unsafe conditions arise.
Use standardized aviation terminology.
Leading by example and promoting a team spirit amongst colleagues.
Respecting the code of conduct and overall behaviour towards the whole NHV team, as well as respecting the Drugs & Alcohol policy.
Line Maintenance
Ensure that all maintenance tasks carried out to helicopters meet the standard of the BCAA/EASA and MOE instructions
Work on aircraft and aircraft components as per Work Order, whilst respecting any restriction applicable.
Ensure all safety regulations are followed
Report any safety hazard and mandatory reportable occurrence via the NHV occurrence reporting system.
Ensure correct following and performing of all maintenance instructions as detailed in the approved maintenance data and ensure correct following of all MOE procedures.
Report if any procedure is inaccurate, incomplete or ambiguous.
Record all performed maintenance in the work file and ensure all tasks are properly certified.
Raise non-routine tasks or defect entries in the system for all unscheduled findings.
Ensure Independent Inspections are performed on all safety critical tasks.
Keep the workspace clean and tidy and keep the tools and equipment in good working order.
Ensure, together with all engineers, a variable & flexible work planning & work roster based on business continuity.
Ensure maintenance is completed before due and within the appropriate time limits to protect the operational needs.
Ensure, in cooperation with NHV Supply Chain and support services that the necessary maintenance material & spare parts are present on site at the planned moment. Ensure, based on pilot & other relevant information, daily problem analysis & problem solving.
Skills and Experience
Experience
- Experience as a Helicopter Hoist Operator
- Minimum 5 years of relevant experience as a licensed engineer.
- Experience working in a line maintenance environment.
- Engineering experience in different aviation companies is a plus.
Skills
- Medical class 2 is required.
- Languages: English (minimum ICAO Level 4)
- EASA Part 66 B1.3 license or B2 license.
- Type rating H175 is preferred
- ICT literate (Rusada Envision knowledge is a plus)
- Good communicator:
- Debriefing with the flight crew
- Briefing of passengers
- Handover of work from/to previous/next shift
- Communication with the maintenance lead
- Able to work independently when performing in a less controlled environment and able to take initiative to improve work and complete tasks within appropriate timeframe.
- Problem solver / logic thinking.
What we offer
NHV Group is an international helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore.
We aim to create a leading position in the helicopter world with a primary focus on Europe and Africa, without compromising our safety. We strive to provide an increasing level of flexibility to our customers. Our services and experience present a refreshing and safe alternative in the industry. NHV cares about it’s people and invests in them to shape the best professionals in the industry.
At NHV, we are dedicated to delivering excellence in the helicopter world. With a commitment to safety, innovation, and customer centricity, we provide reliable services that support a wide range of missions.
Our expertise spans across Oil and Gas, Offshore Wind, Maritime Services, MRO Services, ATO Pilot Training, and P147 Technician Training. We take pride in working with a skilled and passionate team who ensure everything meets the highest industry standards.
Joining NHV means becoming part of an innovative international aviation company that values motivation, enthusiasm, and dynamism. We prioritize our employees, supporting their well-being and growth with comprehensive, role-specific training. Our commitment to fostering a positive work environment includes organizing corporate events and encouraging innovative and critical thinking.

item.title: B1.3 Engineer AW139
item.description:
NHV Group is a European based helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore.
Are you an experienced EASA Part 66 B1.3 Licensed Engineer ready to work in a dynamic offshore helicopter environment? At our Den Helder base, you will play a key role in keeping our AW139 fleet mission-ready and safe. You’ll join a close-knit team of 8 engineers, working in a professional yet supportive environment where safety, teamwork and technical excellence come first.
Please note: Due to operational requirements, candidates must reside within reasonable commuting distance of the base.
Your Role
As a B1.3 Engineer, you are responsible for performing and certifying line maintenance in accordance with approved maintenance data and company procedures. You work closely with fellow engineers under the supervision of the Shift Supervisor, reporting hierarchically to the Base Manager and functionally to the Maintenance Lead. You are more than a certifying engineer — you are a problem solver, safety ambassador and key contributor to operational continuity.
What You’ll Be Doing
Line Maintenance & Technical Excellence
Perform and certify maintenance tasks in accordance with approved maintenance data
Execute work orders while respecting all applicable restrictions
Raise non-routine tasks and defect entries for unscheduled findings
Ensure Independent Inspections on safety-critical tasks
Record and certify all maintenance accurately in the work file
Safety & Compliance
Follow all safety regulations and MOE procedures
Report safety hazards and occurrences via the reporting system
Flag inaccurate, incomplete or unclear procedures
Maintain a clean and orderly workspace with properly maintained tools
Operational Support
Contribute to flexible and efficient work planning
Ensure maintenance is completed within operational deadlines
Coordinate with Supply Chain, Stores and support services to guarantee parts availability
Perform daily technical analysis and problem-solving based on crew feedback
What You Bring
Experience
Minimum 5 years of experience as a licensed engineer
At least 2 years hands-on experience on AW139
Experience in multiple aviation organizations is a plus
Qualifications
EASA Part 66 B1.3 License
Type Rating AW139 (required)
H175 or H160 Type Rating (advantageous)
Skills
Strong troubleshooting and logical thinking skills
Confident communicator (flight crew debriefings & shift handovers)
Able to work independently in less controlled environments
Proactive, flexible and solution-oriented
ICT literate (Rusada Envision knowledge is a plus)
Professional level of English
Living within commuting distance of Den Helder
What We Offer
Join the Thrilling World of Aviation
Become part of an international helicopter services company that combines a youthful mindset with over 25 years of industry expertise. We operate in a highly professional offshore environment where teamwork, safety and technical precision are paramount.
Permanent (indefinite) contract
Competitive salary package
14 days ON / 14 days OFF roster
183 duties per year
Early/Late shift pattern
A supportive team of 8 engineers led by an experienced Shift Supervisor/Maintenance Lead
📲If you’re a certified B1.3 Engineer who thrives in a hands-on, fast-paced helicopter environment and takes pride in delivering safe, high-quality maintenance — we’d love to hear from you.

item.title: B1.3 and B2 H175 Engineer
item.description:
NHV Group is a European based helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore.
Are you an experienced licensed engineer on H175 with a passion for safety and efficiency? Join our team to execute both scheduled and unscheduled crew change flights and Helicopter Hoist Operations (HHO) flights.
The helicopter B1.3 or B2 Engineer plays a critical role ensuring the helicopter is ready and fit for flight and is in an airworthy condition. Performing all required scheduled and unscheduled line maintenance. Safety management and good communication play a crucial role.
You work in a small team of engineers, pilots and hoist operators following the helicopter to different operational locations for crew changes and hoisting on wind farms. You report functionally to the Maintenance Lead who oversees the maintenance and hierarchically to the Base Manager in Esbjerg. You will follow the aircraft in bases in Denmark, the Netherlands, Germany, Poland, Belgium or any other European country the customer requests.
Responsibilities and Main Results Areas
Line Maintenance
- Ensure that all maintenance tasks carried out to helicopters meet the standard of the BCAA/EASA and MOE instructions
- Work on aircraft and aircraft components as per Work Order, whilst respecting any restriction applicable.
- Ensure all safety regulations are followed
- Report any safety hazard and mandatory reportable occurrence via the NHV occurrence reporting system.
- Ensure correct following and performing of all maintenance instructions as detailed in the approved maintenance data and ensure correct following of all MOE procedures.
- Report if any procedure is inaccurate, incomplete or ambiguous.
- Record all performed maintenance in the work file and ensure all tasks are properly certified.
- Raise non-routine tasks or defect entries in the system for all unscheduled findings.
- Ensure Independent Inspections are performed on all safety critical tasks.
- Keep the workspace clean and tidy and keep the tools and equipment in good working order.
- Ensure, together with all engineers, a variable & flexible work planning & work roster based on business continuity.
- Ensure maintenance is completed before due and within the appropriate time limits to protect the operational needs.
- Ensure, in cooperation with NHV Supply Chain and support services that the necessary maintenance material & spare parts are present on site at the planned moment.
- Ensure, based on pilot & other relevant information, daily problem analysis & problem solving.
Hoist Operations
- A combination of an Engineering position and a Helicopter Hoist Operator (HHO) position is a possibility but not a requirement. Refer to the job description HHO for more details.
Skills and Experience
Experience
- Minimum 5 years of relevant experience as a licensed engineer.
- Minimum 2 years of practical experience working on H175 aircraft type.
- Experience working in a line maintenance environment.
- Engineering experience in different aviation companies is a plus.
Skills
- EASA Part 66 B1.3 license or B2 license.

- Good communicator:
- Debriefing of the flight crew

- Problem solver / logic thinking

item.title: H175 Co-Pilot
item.description:
NHV Group is a European based helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore.
To execute scheduled offshore energy flights in the safest and most efficient manner in accordance with aviation regulation and whilst upholding NHV standards.
Responsibilities
Assisting the Pilot-in-Command with the technical preparation of flights including, but not limited to, route planning, fuel calculation, performance limitations and general inspection of the helicopter
Ensuring that all pre and post flight administration is completed accurately under the supervision of the Pilot-in-Command
Assisting the Pilot-in-Command as required with the raising of any reportable incidents and issues post flight via the correct channel.
Conducting pilot duties under the supervision of the Pilot-in-Command whilst fully complying with all safety rules, requirements, limitations and SOPs with full regard to the helicopter type and scope of flight operation
Working alongside the Pilot-in-Command to ensure that all safety systems and equipment are functioning correctly
Maintaining a good working knowledge of NHV Ops Manuals, Notices To Pilots, other related manuals, instructions and procedures
Consistently demonstrating a good awareness of customer service requirements and understanding the client's business & needs, under the supervision of the Chief Pilot.
Performing other assigned flying duties as required
Maintaining familiarity with all relevant national and international air legislation, agreed aviation practices and local procedures.
Maintaining the correct standard of crew discipline, conduct and personal appearance.
Completing tasks allocated by the Chief Pilot as required
Leading by example and promoting a team spirit amongst colleagues.
Respecting the code of conduct and overall behaviour towards the whole NHV team, as well as adhering to the Drugs & Alcohol policy.
Skills & Experience
Skills
Languages:
English Proficiency Level 4 minimum
Sound ICT-knowledge (specific aviation software & administrative programs)
Adheres to all company operational, quality and safety procedures & processes
Flexible (e.g. for double crewing, standby duties ...), a team player, result driven and customer focused
Must be able to obtain a basic Disclosure Scotland certificate.
Experience
Technical requirements:
UK CPL(H) License privileges
Valid UK Class 1 Medical
Valid UK IFR (H) Multi Engine privileges
UK ATPL (H) theory and MCC certificate – essential
Type rating:
H175 - essential
Minimum flight hours:
500 total hours - desirable
500 hours multi-engine on helicopters (may include up to 10% in an approved flight simulator)
100 hours in command (may include up to 10% in a flight simulator). This may be substituted with approved alternative training criteria in accordance with IOGP OHRP.
50 hours on H175 - essential
What we offer
About Us
NHV Group is an international helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore.
We aim to create a leading position in the helicopter world with a primary focus on Europe and Africa, without compromising our safety. We strive to provide an increasing level of flexibility to our customers. Our services and experience present a refreshing and safe alternative in the industry. NHV cares about it’s people and invests in them to shape the best professionals in the industry.
At NHV, we are dedicated to delivering excellence in the helicopter world. With a commitment to safety, innovation, and customer centricity, we provide reliable services that support a wide range of missions.
Our expertise spans across Oil and Gas, Offshore Wind, Maritime Services, MRO Services, ATO Pilot Training, and P147 Technician Training. We take pride in working with a skilled and passionate team who ensure everything meets the highest industry standards.
Our Values
Safety – We promote a Just Culture of trust, respect and accountability in safety, free from fear of retaliation.
Integrity – We take pride in our work, act responsibly, and deliver high-quality results on time to support NHV and its partners.
Ownership – We uphold the highest ethical standards, follow all regulations, and foster fairness and trust while continuously improving together.
Partnership – We value strong partnerships and treat our people as equals, working with fairness, respect, and a drive for top performance.
Joining NHV means becoming part of an international, dynamic, and professional environment where growth and development are encouraged.
What We Offer
Joining NHV means becoming part of an innovative international aviation company that values motivation, enthusiasm, and dynamism. We prioritize our employees, supporting their well-being and growth with comprehensive, role-specific training. Our commitment to fostering a positive work environment includes organizing corporate events and encouraging innovative and critical thinking.
In addition to a casual dress code and flexible working days, we offer a range of competitive employee benefits designed to enhance your career and well-being.
- Matched pension plan
- Private medical insurance
- Employee assistance program
- Life insurance
- Cycle to work scheme
NHV is committed to providing equality and fairness and ensuring that no individual is subjected to discrimination on the grounds of any of the following characteristics: age, disability, gender, pregnancy, family status, gender reassignment, sexual orientation, race, ethnic origin, religion or belief.

item.title: Mechanic - Aberdeen
item.description:
NHV Group is a European based helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore.
The Mechanic will work in a team and is responsible for the maintenance of the helicopters. They report to the Chief Engineer of the base.
NHV Group is an international helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore.
We aim to create a leading position in the helicopter world with a primary focus on Europe and Africa, without compromising our safety. We strive to provide an increasing level of flexibility to our customers. Our services and experience present a refreshing and safe alternative in the industry. NHV cares about it’s people and invests in them to shape the best professionals in the industry.
At NHV, we are dedicated to delivering excellence in the helicopter world. With a commitment to safety, innovation, and customer centricity, we provide reliable services that support a wide range of missions.
Responsibilities
Line Maintenance
- Basic tasks in Line Maintenance, in compliance with the requirements as described in the regulations: refueling, towing, baggage loading and other line activities;
- Ensure that all these line maintenance tasks carried out to helicopters meet the standard of the UK CAA and M.O.E. instructions;
- Ensure that all maintenance is completed within the appropriate time scales to ensure provision of a serviceable aircraft to meet the operational need;
- Assist the licensed engineers in trouble shooting;
- Assist the licensed engineers in correct handling of maintenance tasks – final check to be done by the licensed engineers.
- Ensure, in cooperation with NHV Supply Chain, Store that the necessary maintenance material and spare parts are present on site at the planned moment: material in service car, consumables, limited service stock, work material, material needed for normal day-to-day line maintenance, exceptional material supplied for exceptional line maintenance or small repair tasks;
- Report daily to the licensed engineer on completion of tasks.
Base Maintenance
- Comply with the requirements as described in the regulations;
- Ensure that all maintenance tasks carried out to helicopters meet the standard of the UK CAA and M.O.E. instructions;
- Ensure that all maintenance is completed within the appropriate time scales to ensure provision of serviceable aircraft to meet the operational need;
- Ensure, in cooperation with NHV Supply Chain, Store & MCC that the necessary maintenance material & spare parts are present on site at the planned moment.
Skills and Experience
Experience
- Practical experience in maintenance in either line or base maintenance (preferably on helicopters) is a must
Skills
- ICT literate (specific aviation software & MS Office)
- Languages: English (professional level)
- Ability to follow company procedures and processes (operational, quality, safety …)
- Easy in learning new procedures and processes: motivated to study to obtain the right experience and knowledge in helicopter maintenance
Solution finder but with eye for detail and taking into account all regulations and procedures
What we offer
Joining NHV means becoming part of an innovative international aviation company that values motivation, enthusiasm, and dynamism. We prioritize our employees, supporting their well-being and growth with comprehensive, role-specific training. Our commitment to fostering a positive work environment includes organizing corporate events and encouraging innovative and critical thinking.
In addition to a casual dress code and flexible working days, we offer a range of competitive employee benefits designed to enhance your career and well-being.
- Matched pension plan
- Private medical insurance
- Employee assistance program
- Life insurance
- Cycle to work scheme

About NHV
NHV Group is an international helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore.

item.title: Helicopter mechanic - Lanvéoc
item.description:
NHV Group is a European based helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore.
As a Mechanic at our base in Lanvéoc, you'll join a small but highly skilled and enthusiast team, committed to excellence.
Responsibilities & areas
Maintenance tasks on aircraft and components in accordance with issued work orders, applicable manuals, and approved maintenance data.
Carry out activities such as:
Disassembly & reassembly
Rectify defects
Component replacements and system adjustments
Ensure all safety regulations and procedures are followed at all times;
Immediately report safety hazards or mandatory occurrences via the NHV occurrence reporting system;
Follow all MOE procedures and notify the Chief Engineer/Maintenance Lead if instructions or processes are unclear, incomplete, or ambiguous;
Record all performed maintenance accurately and clearly in the work file;
Raise defect entries or non-routine tasks for any findings discovered during inspections;
Support Licensed Engineers during troubleshooting and functional checks;
Maintain a clean, safe, and organized working environment, including proper care of tools and ground equipment;
Contribute to a flexible and efficient planning;
Help ensure that planned maintenance is completed within appropriate timeframes to support operational needs.
Skills & Experience
At NHV, we are dedicated to deliver excellence in the helicopter world.
With a commitment to safety, innovation, and customer centricity, we provide reliable services that support a wide range of missions.
We expect you to contribute to this level of service and trust that you will bring the following competencies to strengthen the team:
Good communication skills to work together with direct colleagues, store keepers, CAMO engineers, etc. and for handover work;
Demonstrated experience in base and line maintenance operations;
Strong understanding of EASA regulations and safety protocols;
Accuracy and reliability;
Flexibility;
Easily combine working in a team and the ability to work independently.
Languages:
English & French: fluent, professional level (speaking, reading, writing);
Only candidates who meet the legal right to live and work in France will be considered for this position.
What we offer
Step into an exciting career in an international helicopter services company that combines a youthful spirit with over 25 years of industry expertise.
When you join our team, you can count on a permanent (indefinite) contract and a competitive salary package, supplemented with meal & gift vouchers, etc.

item.title: B2 Engineer - Lanvéoc
item.description:
NHV Group is a European based helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore.
We’re looking for an experienced B2 engineer to join our dedicated team at our base in Lanvéoc, France. You'll be part of a small but highly skilled and enthusiast team, committed to excellence.
Responsibilities & areas
Perform base & line maintenance on aircraft and components as per work orders;
Perform effective troubleshooting based on pilot reports and system indications;
Ensure that all maintenance tasks are carried out, following all approved maintenance data, procedures (MOE), and airworthiness regulations;
Ensure that all maintenance is completed within the appropriate time scales to have a serviceable aircraft that meets the operational need;
Accurately certify completed tasks and ensure proper documentation in the work pack;
Ensure proper handling, segregation, and documentation of removed/installed components;
Adhere to safety standards; report hazards and occurrences via the NHV reporting system;
Ensure briefing & debriefing of the Chief Engineer and/or Maintenance Lead; Report daily on the maintenance situation; Inform and update the CE/ML on the maintenance situation;
Certify B2 category maintenance activities in accordance with the regulations;
Work independently when performing in a less controlled environment and take initiative to improve work, show flexibility within the team to complete tasks within appropriate time scales.

At NHV, we are dedicated to deliver excellence in the helicopter world. With a commitment to safety, innovation, and customer centricity, we provide reliable services that support a wide range of missions. EASA Part 66 B2 License (upload copy when applying);
AS365 N3 type rated;
Good communication skills to work together with direct colleagues, store keepers, CAMO engineers, etc. and for handover work;
Demonstrated experience in base and line maintenance operations;
Strong understanding of EASA regulations and safety protocols;
Accuracy and reliability;
Flexibility;
Easily combine working in a team and the ability to work independently.
Languages:
English & French: fluent, professional level (speaking, reading, writing);
Only candidates who meet the legal right to live and work in France will be considered for this position.
What we offer
Step into an exciting career in an international helicopter services company that combines a youthful spirit with over 25 years of industry expertise.
When you join our team, you can count on a permanent (indefinite) contract and a competitive salary package, supplemented with meal & gift vouchers, etc.
